revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Jinraku-ji]][[fr:Jinraku-ji]][[ja:秦楽寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:田原本町秦庄 秦楽寺 Jinrakuji, Hatanoshō 2011.11.13 - panoramio.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tawaramoto|qid=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.543533|N|135.792528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Use dmy dates|date=July 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Jinraku-ji
秦楽寺 | native_name = | image = 田原本町秦庄 秦楽寺 Jinrakuji, Hatanoshō 2011.11.13 - panoramio.jpg | image_size = 250 | alt = | caption = | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 267 Hatanoshō, {{ill|Tawaramoto, Nara|en|Tawaramoto, Nara|qid=Q1203159|lt=Tawaramoto-chō|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|Shiki District, Nara|en|Shiki District, Nara|qid=Q1155036|lt=Shiki District|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|37.4|N|135|47|31.6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shingon Risshu|en|Shingon Risshu|qid=Q580527|lt=Shingon Ritsu|1=Shingon Risshu}} | deity = {{ill|Thousand-armed Kannon|fr|Thousand-armed Kannon|ja|千手観音|lt=Thousand-Armed Kannon}} (Thousand-armed Avalokiteśvara) | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = {{ill|Hata no Kawakatsu|en|Hata no Kawakatsu|qid=Q5680877|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}} | year_completed = 5th century
1759 (restoration) }} {{Nihongo|'''Jinraku-ji'''|秦楽寺}} is a {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhist|1=Buddhism}} temple in the town of {{ill|Tawaramoto, Nara|en|Tawaramoto, Nara|qid=Q1203159|lt=Tawaramoto|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is located in Tawaramoto near {{ill|Kasanui Station|en|Kasanui Station|qid=Q6374013|lt=Kasanui Station|1=Kasanui Station}}. It was commissioned by [[:en:Prince Shotoku|Prince Shotoku]] in the 5th century and built by {{ill|Hata no Kawakatsu|en|Hata no Kawakatsu|qid=Q5680877|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}}. The Great Hall contains a {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|qid=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva|1=Bodhisattva}} while outside there is also a [[Shinto]] shrine with a [[torii]] and a pond in the shape of a Chinese character.{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=DvhOAQAAIAAJ|title=Buddha his life and teaching "Buddhist Temples" p.50|accessdate=2016-07-13|publisher=Mahavir & Sons Publisher|year=2009|author=Neeraj Gautam|isbn=9788183772471}} ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist|colwidth=30em}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Buddhist-temple-stub}} [[en:Jinraku-ji]] {{Commons category|Jinrakuji}} [[fr:Jinraku-ji]] [[ja:秦楽寺]] {{wikidata link|Q3179237}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture|Jinraku-ji]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Prince Shōtoku]] [[Category:Shingon Ritsu temples]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
